components.name: Sodium acetate
components.evidence: Sodium acetate was added to a concentration of 5.0 mM as the sole source of electrons and carbon.

media.base_medium: Modified MR-1 (Shewanella) phosphate-buffered mineral medium of Yoon, Sanford & Loffler 2013 AEM (ref 31); pH ~7.0, phosphate-buffered, N2-flushed with trace N2O as electron acceptor

sources_chased.what: Table S1 (1000X trace metal solution) + Table S2 (1000X vitamin stock) in the paper's own Supplementary Information (41396_2022_1260_MOESM1_ESM.docx)
sources_chased.how: EuropePMC supplementaryFiles zip -> extracted DOCX word/document.xml

sources_chased.what: base-medium reference 31 (Yoon, Sanford & Loffler 2013 AEM, PMC3623185) to confirm the MR-1 mineral base and its N/P/salt scheme
added: confirmed phosphate-buffered basal salts (NaCl, NH4Cl, KH2PO4, K2HPO4) + trace metal + vitamin stock design

missing_before: All 9 vitamins (4-aminobenzoic acid, D-biotin, nicotinic acid, Ca-pantothenate, pyridoxine-HCl, folic acid, alpha-lipoic acid, thiamine-HCl, cyanocobalamine/B12)
Trace elements Ni (NiCl2), Se (H2SeO3), B (H3BO3), Mo (Na2MoO4), W (Na2WO4), Al (AlK(SO4)x)
Explicit identities/concentrations of the Fe, Mn, Zn, Cu, Co, Ca trace-metal salts (previously only generic ions)
media.notes: Complete recipe recovered from the paper's own Supplementary Tables S1 (trace metals) and S2 (vitamins); both are 1000X stocks added at 1 mL per L, so listed final concentrations are stock amount/100 mL divided by 100. NOTE: the recipe contains NO magnesium salt (neither the base nor Table S1 includes Mg), so the 'Magnesium (Mg2+, from trace metal solution)' entry in the prior current_components is spurious and should be dropped. Culture was microaerobic (dissolved O2 could not be reduced below ~10 uM) but effectively N2-flushed with trace N2O (~200 ppmv) as the terminal electron acceptor; no chemical reductant (cysteine/sulfide) and no bicarbonate buffer are used - buffering is by phosphate at pH ~7.
